members.name: Bitbiashara
members.tagline: Empowering Tena estate entrepreneurs with Bitcoin education and help unlock global opportunities for local businesses
members.description: BitBiashara is short for Bitcoin and Biashara. Biashara is a Swahili word for Business. We are all about showing Kenyan businesses and entrepreneurs that Bitcoin can indeed save all their business through Bitcoin. We've studied Bitcoin, we still do, and we've concluded that Bitcoin is a tool powerful enough to save and empower entrepreneurs in Africa, saving them from the challenges of the current fiat and broken system which is inflationary, highly bureaucratic, unfair especially to the medium and small businessman Bitbiashara presents Bitcoin for its original purpose of value transfer, value ownership and Freedom.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

We are pioneering the future of business in Tena estate Nairobi by empowering entrepreneurs with the available tools, knowledge, and opportunities to leverage Bitcoin in their operations. BitBiashara is committed to introducing Bitcoin, demonstrating Bitcoin transforming with the knowledge of better money, and hefty fees reduction.

Our Mission

To educate, empower, and support entrepreneurs in our community embrace Bitcoin. By doing so, we aim to help them enhance their business operations, reduce transaction costs, and access global opportunities and build wealth for the future through Bitcoin. Through practical education and the demonstrations of user-friendly platforms, we strive to make Bitcoin accessible to everyone, regardless of their technological expertise.
members.how_started: The journey begun with me the founder joining Bitcoin dada, understanding how valuable bitcoin really is and realising that people have the wrong information about bitcoin. I set on a journey to change the narrative and being an entrepreneur, i connected better with fellow entrepreneurs.
members.milestones: We have managed to structure 4 basics courses, Bitcoin basics for beginners, Bitcoin for business, Bitcoin For digital creators and Bitcoin for freelancers, the end product will be totally open-source. We use our own material so that we can connect better with the clientele by speaking their language. We have managed to educate 4 cohorts of business people, with 23 onboarded clients and 11 captured on camera, 2 merchants gathering meetups. 16 of these merchants are Tena estate.

members.name: Afribit Kibera
members.tagline: Bringing financial inclusion to Kibera through Bitcoin education, merchant onboarding, and sustainable community programs
members.description: Welcome to Afribit Kibera – A pioneering project by Afribit, designed to bring financial inclusion to the heart of Kibera's informal settlement. In a community where traditional financial systems are out of reach for many, Afribit Kibera is leading the charge by educating and onboarding local merchants to embrace the power of Bitcoin. By fostering a circular economy, we aim to empower Kibera's self-employed and casual laborers, unlocking financial freedom for businesses that have been excluded from centralized banking.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

Afribit’s Kibera emphasizes financial empowerment and inclusion for marginalized communities, particularly in Kibera informal settlement, by creating a sustainable Bitcoin Circular Economy.

Our Mission

Afribit Kibera focuses on leveraging Bitcoin to bridge financial access gaps, empowering communities through education, merchant onboarding, and offering Bitcoin earning economic opportunities through upcycling and waste management programs
members.how_started: In 2019, Afribit's journey began with a vision to promote financial inclusion in underserved communities, starting with Kibera, Kenya. The idea was sparked by Yogi Golle, a co-founder of Afribit, who was a passionate supporter of the Ronnie Fund—a charitable initiative. Inspired by the story of Bitcoin Beach in El Salvador, Yogi recognized Bitcoin's potential to transform lives in marginalized communities. He introduced Ronnie, a key figure behind the Ronnie Fund, to the world of Bitcoin, and their partnership quickly grew into something much larger. Motivated by the success of Bitcoin Beach, Yogi sought to further support the Ronnie Fund by focusing on Bitcoin donations. In late 2019, Yogi and Ronnie took their commitment to the next level by offering educational opportunities to students in the Kibera community. They recruited local students to take a Bitcoin 101 course, as well as a Bitcoin programming and blockchain course at Ivan Tech Academy. Yogi sponsored the courses, providing resources like laptops for students to learn and work from a local hub established by Ronnie. This hub became a space where students could explore Bitcoin and blockchain technology, and under Yogi's remote mentorship, they gained skills in Bitcoin usage, programming, and even secured jobs in the space. The success of these students motivated Yogi and Ronnie to expand their efforts. Seeing the growing need for financial inclusion in Kibera, where many people were excluded from traditional banking systems, they decided to scale up their initiative. Leveraging Ronnie’s strong connections within the Kibera community, they identified a unique opportunity to use Bitcoin as a solution to financial exclusion. They officially launched the Afribit Kibera project, a community-driven initiative designed to introduce Bitcoin-based solutions to the people. Yogi encouraged the team to apply for a Bitcoin Beach grant and raise funds on the Geyser platform, which gave the initiative a crucial boost. The movement has been growing organically, inspiring locals to start their own Bitcoin initiatives.
members.milestones: Onboarded 40+ merchants transacting in Bitcoin: Expanding Bitcoin’s use in local businesses, fostering adoption and inclusivity in the economy. Launched recycling programs rewarding participants in SATs: Encouraging sustainable practices while introducing the community to Bitcoin through rewards. Women upcycling project where young women upcycle old fabric into decors and mats Merchants and youth saving in Bitcoin: Promoting long-term wealth preservation and financial independence by saving in Bitcoin, protecting against inflation. Empowering youth through Bitcoin education & employment: Providing bitcoin education and job opportunities in waste management, boosting skills and income. Building community trust and understanding of Bitcoin’s role in financial inclusion: Demystifying Bitcoin and showing its practical use for broader financial access in underserved communities.

members.name: Bitcoin Githurai
members.tagline: Building a thriving Bitcoin circular economy in Toez Githurai through merchant education and community adoption

members.name: Bitcoin Dua
members.tagline: Empowering Agbozume's youth with Bitcoin literacy and financial independence to build a self-sustaining circular economy
members.description: Bitcoin Dua is a groundbreaking initiative started in Agbozume, Ghana, aimed at fostering financial independence among the youth through Bitcoin literacy. Our mission is to educate and empower the youth with essential financial literacy skills, emphasizing the benefits and applications of Bitcoin. Our goal is to create a self-sustaining circular economy where everyone understands and utilizes Bitcoin's value and potential
members.vision_mission: Our vision at Bitcoin Dua is to create a thriving and self-sustaining ecosystem where every individual, especially the youth, is equipped with the knowledge and tools to harness the potential of Bitcoin. We envision a future where Bitcoin catalyzes financial independence, innovation, and inclusive economic growth, fostering a community empowered to shape its destiny.
members.milestones: Bitcoin Dua has made great strides in driving Bitcoin adoption and digital skills education. We’ve onboarded over 20 merchants, trained more than 200 kids in coding and digital literacy, and empowered local businesses to accept Bitcoin. Through meetups and workshops, we educate communities about Bitcoin’s benefits while fostering a circular economy. With the support of Bitcoin, we’re preparing young minds for future jobs and expanding financial freedom. A major milestone is the recent launch of BitSpenda, a platform that makes Bitcoin spending seamless across Africa. Our journey continues as we grow our merchant network, reach more learners, and drive greater Bitcoin adoption. 🚀⚡

members.name: Bitcoin Chama
members.tagline: Empowering rural Kenya through accessible Bitcoin projects like beekeeping and farming, building financial independence
members.description: Bitcoin chama is a rural based Bitcoin community with a mission to build a self-reliant Bitcoin circular ecosystem. How do we achieve this? We empower young people and our community economically by introducing accessible, low-resource projects that require minimal time and space to implement. We then encourage them to save their earnings in Bitcoin, promoting financial independence and long-term sustainability
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

is to have a self reliant community that lives daily on Bitcoin in rural kenya.

Our Mission

is to use Bitcoin as a tool to change people lives in my community by empowering them economically through various projects and save what we get from this projects in Bitcoin. We are building on Bitcoin.
members.how_started: Initially, I didn’t know much about Bitcoin. But I had a friend who worked at Boda Boda. He was trying to save up his earnings to buy iron sheets and build a better house for his family. Since he didn't get enough money from Boda Boda, he saved whatever little he could in a bank, a savings group. Over the course of two years, when he finally went to withdraw his money, he realized that the price of iron sheets had increased by almost 100%. Originally, he had planned to buy 30 iron sheets, but by the time he withdrew his savings, his money could no longer afford that many. For someone who works so hard to earn a living, this was a major setback. That’s when I started thinking—how can we solve this problem? At the time, I didn’t know much about Bitcoin or how money really works. But seeing my friend struggle, I realized that the real issue wasn’t just rising prices, but the financial system itself. Later that year, I was introduced to Bitcoin, and it suddenly clicked! If my friend had saved in Bitcoin instead of fiat, his purchasing power wouldn’t have been eroded. In fact, he might have even been able to afford nails, labor costs, or additional materials. That realization led me to create Bitcoin Chama community to help people save in Bitcoin and protect their hard earned money
members.milestones: We have: 1. Piped free clean water for people in our community 2. Weve constructed and supplied beehives to people in our community to supplement what they earn 3. We have a community gym. 4. We have constructed a chicken coop and bought a few chicken for our community chama.

members.name: Bitcoin Ekasi
members.tagline: Building a Bitcoin circular economy in Mossel Bay township through youth empowerment and merchant adoption
members.description: Bitcoin Ekasi is an extension of The Surfer Kids Non-Profit. The Surfer Kids is a registered non-profit organization, founded in 2010. The NPO is 100% donation-based and works to create youth-empowerment in an impoverished South African township. Similar to what Bitcoin Beach did in El Zonte, El Salvador, Bitcoin Ekasi aims to use a well established community platform (The Surfer Kids NPO) to create a Bitcoin economy in the township already serviced by that non-profit organization.
members.how_started: The Surfer Kids’ existing program operates 5 days a week, all year round, and is run by coaches from the same township community. Bitcoin Ekasi pays these coaches’ salaries with Bitcoin, while simultaneously on-boarding township vendors to accept Bitcoin as payment. This allows the coaches to spend their SATs buying groceries in the township, thereby introducing Bitcoin into the township economy. All of this happens over the Lightning Network. Bitcoin’s layer-two scaling solution, designed to expand the transaction throughput of the Bitcoin network to a truly global scale.

members.name: BitKwa
members.tagline: Building Ilorin's Bitcoin economy through education, merchant adoption, and peer-to-peer trade networks
members.description: The BitKwa Circular Economy, Ilorin Pilot is a community-first initiative designed to build a living Bitcoin economy in Ilorin, Kwara State, Nigeria. Through education, merchant adoption, and peer-to-peer trade, BitKwa is empowering residents to earn, spend, and thrive using Bitcoin. This pilot marks the beginning of a scalable model, where communities across Kwara can achieve financial sovereignty and inclusion, one Bitcoin circle at a time.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

To build a financially sovereign society where individuals and communities in Africa live, work, and trade freely through sustainable Bitcoin circular economies.

Our Mission

To empower communities in Ilorin and beyond by driving real-world Bitcoin adoption through education, merchant integration, and circular trade networks, creating a self-sustaining economy powered by knowledge, innovation, and financial freedom.
members.how_started: The BitKwa Circular Economy was born out of a simple question, what if communities could actually live on Bitcoin, not just learn about it? After months of educating hundreds of people through the BitKwa Bitcoin Diploma, it became clear that knowledge alone wasn’t enough. Many learners understood Bitcoin but had nowhere to earn or spend it. Merchants were curious but lacked guidance. A few early adopters started trading peer-to-peer, but without structure, it couldn’t grow. That’s when BitKwa decided to take the next step, to turn education into real-world impact. Ilorin, Kwara State, the home of BitKwa became the natural starting point. It’s where the vision of a Bitcoin-powered local economy began to take shape: one where people could earn, save, and spend in Bitcoin, all within their own community. What started as a small spark of education has now evolved into a full-fledged mission, to build Kwara first living Bitcoin circular economy, right here in Ilorin, and replicate it across the State.

members.name: Bitcoin NextGen
members.tagline: Bitcoin circular economy project in Nigeria

members.name: Bitcoin Ekiti
members.tagline: Spreading Bitcoin adoption across Ekiti State through education and helping merchants accept Bitcoin
members.description: BitcoinEkiti is a circular economy that focus majorly on ensuring that Bitcoin education, Adoption and integration is well established in Ekiti state Nigeria.
members.vision_mission: BitcoinEkiti was created to ensure that the gospel of bitcoin is being pass accros to the people of Ekiti State, Nigeria. Our goal is to build a bitcoin circular economy where we help merchants accepts bitcoin for everyday transactions.

members.name: Bitcoin Ikorodu
members.tagline: Empowering Ikorodu's youth and businesses through Bitcoin education, merchant onboarding, and community events
members.description: We are building a Local Bitcoin Circular Economy in Ikorodu, Lagos, Nigeria.
members.vision_mission: Build a self-sustaining Bitcoin circular economy in Ikorodu, where community members earn, save, and spend in Bitcoin daily, empowering local businesses, youth, and institutions.
members.milestones: Hosted a Bitcoin in School Program in Ikorodu, Lagos, Nigeria, hosting about 80 school students. PoW Video Orange-Pilled and onboarded Merchants, including a Barber Shop that now accepts Bitcoin Payments. PoW Video Creating a community where Bitcoin can be Spent Locally. PoW Video Hosted a Bitcoin Christmas Kids event: PoW Post

members.name: Bitcoin Dombo
members.tagline: Empowering Mozambique's youth to build sustainable businesses through Bitcoin education and entrepreneurship training
members.description: Bitcoin Dombo is a Bitcoin Circular Economy located in Mozambique, created to empower young people to become entrepreneurs through small businesses that accept and use Bitcoin as a means of payment and savings. Bitcoin Dombo is under Maputo Skate and Education Umbrella.
members.vision_mission: Empower young people and local communities to start sustainable small businesses using Bitcoin as a means of payment, savings, and investment. Promote financial and technological education, encouraging autonomy, creativity, and collaboration within a Bitcoin-based circular economy.

members.name: BitJR Academy
members.tagline: Building Bitcoin circular economy in Zambia through education and local merchant onboarding

members.name: Bitcoin Victoria Falls
members.tagline: Building a thriving Bitcoin ecosystem in Livingstone through education, meetups, and merchant onboarding programs
members.description: Bitcoin Victoria Falls is a Bitcoin circular economy initiative in the southern part of Zambia, dedicated to education, merchant adoption, and community building. The project aims to create a thriving Bitcoin ecosystem where individuals and businesses can seamlessly transact using Bitcoin. Through educational programs, Bitcoin meetups, and hands-on merchant onboarding efforts, Bitcoin Victoria Falls empowers locals with the knowledge and tools needed to understand and use Bitcoin. The initiative also works closely with business owners to integrate Bitcoin payments into their operations, fostering a sustainable and practical use of Bitcoin in everyday transactions.
members.vision_mission: Rekindling hope in southern part of Zambia.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Victoria Falls was born out of the inspiration of Bitcoin Beach in El Salvador and other similar Bitcoin circular economies. In late 2022, Adam visited El Zonte, El Salvador, and saw the impact of the Bitcoin Beach project. Inspired by its success, he wanted to bring a similar model to Zambia. He saw potential in the Free Haven Community Initiative in Livingstone, which shared similarities with The Hope House in El Zonte. Adam reached out to Samantha Knaub, the Founder of Free Haven, whom he knew through his sister. Having supported Free Haven for over six years, he was familiar with the community’s needs. Samantha introduced him to Free Haven’s President, Forest Hall, who was open to the idea of a Bitcoin Circular Economy. Together with Jason Peck, they began meeting regularly in early 2023 to lay the groundwork. By late 2023, they began searching for a teacher to help educate the community about Bitcoin. With the support of Ndesanjo Macho ("Ndesa"), the Bitcoin for Fairness Zambia organizer, and Anita Posch, the Founder of Bitcoin for Fairness, they put the right team in place. In 2024, Bitcoin Victoria Falls officially launched, founded by Adam and Humphrey, with Humphrey as the community leader. The project has focused on education and merchant adoption, starting with a 5-week Mi Primer Bitcoin / My First Bitcoin course that is open to the public in the area. The course is being held in a rented classroom. To further promote Bitcoin adoption, monthly meetups were introduced, hosted at Jollyboys Backpackers, a local business that accepts Bitcoin. These meetups provide a space for education, networking, and growing the Bitcoin community. With each passing week, Bitcoin Victoria Falls continues to expand. Funding supports teacher payments, Bitcoin workbooks, graduation ceremonies, and activities like helping students set up Lightning wallets. What started as an idea inspired by El Zonte has now grown into a real movement, bringing Bitcoin adoption to Livingstone, Zambia.

members.name: Bitcoin Anambra
members.tagline: Leading Bitcoin adoption in Anambra State through community education and building a thriving circular economy
members.description: Bitcoin Anambra is leading the charge in driving Bitcoin adoption and building a thriving Bitcoin circular economy in Anambra State, Nigeria.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

1. Educate the Community: Conduct educational campaigns to raise awareness about Bitcoin and its benefits. 2. Promote Bitcoin Adoption: Encourage merchants in the Onitsha Main Market and other key locations to accept Bitcoin. 3. Build a Circular Economy: Create a sustainable ecosystem where Bitcoin is widely used for daily transactions within Anambra State.

Our Mission

1. Bitcoin Educational Meetups/Events: Organize regular events to educate locals about Bitcoin. 2. Outdoor Publicity: Utilize outdoor advertising in strategic locations to promote Bitcoin awareness across the state. 3. Workshops at Tech Hubs: Conduct workshops at selected technology hubs in the state to engage tech-savvy individuals and entrepreneurs. 4. Orange Pilling of Merchants: Directly engage merchants in Onitsha Main Market to demonstrate how Bitcoin can streamline their transactions. 5. Sats Giveaway: Distribute small amounts of Bitcoin (satoshis) to locals to familiarize them with its use and to also spend at the merchants we've onboarded.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Anambra started as a way to educate locals in Anambra State about Bitcoin and how they can use it beyond just investing. Our goal is to build a Bitcoin circular economy by helping businesses accept Bitcoin for everyday transactions.

members.name: Bitcoin Sisonke
members.tagline: Building Johannesburg's Bitcoin circular economy through education and merchant adoption
members.description: Bitcoin Sisonke started as an Bitcoin education initiative that hails from the West Rand, Johannesburg, where we conducted Bitcoin educational programs and meet ups for people interested in learning about Bitcoin. As we dug deeper in the Bitcoin rabbit hole we established a Bitcoin Sisonke circular economy. Our knowledge and experience has inspired us to build a sustainable Bitcoin circular economy where we encourage merchant to start accepting bitcoin as an alternative form of payment. We established strategic partnership with our local media entity to accelerate Bitcoin adoption and awareness around our community.

members.name: Calabar Bitcoin Club
members.tagline: Building Bitcoin adoption in Calabar through education, merchant onboarding, and a legal fight for Bitcoin rights
members.description: The Calabar Bitcoin Club was founded to introduce a parallel financial ecosystem where residents could address their daily financial challenges, which include: inflation, exclusion, censorship, remittance issues and other limitations of traditional financial systems. Bitcoin presents a solution, not just as money; it's about building a brighter money future for Calabar. We intend to Achieve this by promoting Bitcoin Adotpion via a Circular Economy, Educatoin and Skills development.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

To create a future for Calabar where individuals and businesses can confidently explore Bitcoin as a tool for money freedom, financial sovereignty and economic resilence. We hope to inspire the replication of successfull bitcoin circular economies in Nigeria and Africa.

Our Mission

The Calabar Bitcoin Club is dedicated to fostering widespread understanding and adpotion of Bitcoin across Calabar by educating residents, supporting local merchants and building a strong circular economy on Bitcoin.
members.how_started: The club emerged out of passion from the bitcoin adoption work already being carried out by our founder James Otudor. Aggregating all his adoption work into a one powerful force gave birth to the Calabar Bitcoin Club.
members.milestones: There are lots of Milestons achieved, let's list a few. Creation of a Secondary School Bitcoin Club in Local Public Schools in Calabar Supporting our founder James Otudor in the Bitcoin vs Nigeria court case; where he is currently suing the Nigerian government and several federal agencies who have infringed on the rights of Nigerian residents to own, use and trade bitcoin, as well as demanding that those rights be protected. Held three local meetups with 250+ people in attendance. Held a The Bitcoin Halving Festival which attracted over 10,000+ participants Hosted 4 sessions of 3omins Bitcoin only Radio programm. Published 2 Bitcoin articles on multiple national newspapers. Restructured the club to be self sustaining and empleyed 7 volunteers. Rented the Calabar Bitcoin Club Centre where physical businesses that supports a circular econmy will be operated. Onboarded 2 business to start accepting bitcoin as payments. Partnered with iPayBTC to bring bitcoin education to the campus community of University of Calabar, Nigeria

members.name: Bitcoin Aves
members.tagline: Building Bitcoin adoption in rural Akatsi, Ghana through merchant onboarding, youth education, and community empowerment
members.description: Bitcoin Aves is a grassroots initiative in Ghana dedicated to fostering a Bitcoin-only circular economy. We focus on onboarding merchants, equipping local businesses to accept Bitcoin for everyday transactions, and providing education to empower individuals to use Bitcoin confidently. Through meet-up sessions, community outreach, and practical adoption strategies, we aim to create a sustainable ecosystem where value flows freely, independent of centralized systems. Our vision is simple: a thriving community built on sound money, shared growth, and financial freedom.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

To see Akatsi become a leading example of Bitcoin-powered living in rural Africa, where shops accept sats, farmers sell their produce for sats, youths earn in sats, and schools teach Bitcoin as part of everyday life. We dream of a Bitcoin Circular Economy, built from the ground up and owned by the people.

Our Mission

To empower individuals and communities across Akatsi and beyond through Bitcoin Education, practical adoption, and local leadership so they can take control of their financial future with dignity and confidence.
members.how_started: In late December 2024, in a small Farming community called Akatsi, located in the Volta Region of Ghana, a quiet revolution is underway. This revolution is not about politics or protests but about Bitcoin, which is giving everyday people a new sense of hope, ownership, transparency, and financial freedom. It began with a few teachers, students and merchants asking a power questions: "What if we could teach our people how to save, earn, and spend sound money?" From that spark in late March 2025, Akatsi's first Bitcoin project - "Bitcoin Aves" was born.
members.milestones: Bitcon-only Initiative: We are strictly rooted in Bitcoin; no altcoins, no distractions. Bitcoin Education: We provide educational outreach to community youths through social and sports-related gatherings. Merchant Onboarding: Educate merchants and business owners to understand, adopt, and accept Bitcoin (sats) in their merchandise, and guide them in configuring and setting up wallets. Meetup Sessions & Consultation: We deliver insightful guidance and foster meaningful community connections by hosting engaging meetup sessions, empowering individuals through shared learning and collaboration.

members.name: BTC Shule
members.tagline: Transforming Winteko village, Burundi through Bitcoin education, financial inclusion, and circular economy building
members.description: BTC Shule is transforming Winteko, Burundi, into a Bitcoin-powered community through a circular economy. By providing access to financial inclusion, renewable energy, and education, this initiative empowers local residents to become financially self-sufficient using Bitcoin as a tool for social transformation.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

The of BTC Shule is to build a self-sustaining Bitcoin-based economy in Winteko. The

Our Mission

is to use Bitcoin and solar power to educate, empower, and uplift the community, providing access to essential services like electricity and financial inclusion, which are otherwise out of reach.
members.how_started: Inspired by Bitcoin’s success in other communities worldwide like Bitcoin Ekasi, BTC Shule began in Winteko as a response to the village’s lack of financial institutions, electricity, and access to education. By integrating Bitcoin with solar energy, BTC Shule aims to create a thriving, decentralized community that is financially and environmentally sustainable.
members.milestones: BTC Shule's educational milestones include launching Bitcoin education sessions for all households within the first three months, followed by the establishment of a Bitcoin-based rewards system to encourage community participation in educational programs. In the short term, BTC Shule will focus on expanding the scope of financial and Bitcoin education, supporting local entrepreneurs with Bitcoin-backed grants to stimulate learning in business development. Over the long term, BTC Shule aims to refine its educational programs, ensuring they are sustainable and effective in fostering financial literacy and entrepreneurship within the community.

members.name: Bitcoin Babies
members.tagline: Empowering Kenyan mothers with babies through Bitcoin stipends and a circular economy of 15+ local merchant partners
members.description: Bitcoin Babies is a foundation that aims to support mothers with babies under the age of one. It helps women integrate Bitcoin into daily life, creating new economic opportunities in a system that often excludes them. What started as a simple financial support program has grown into a circular economy where local merchants accept Bitcoin as payment. By using Bitcoin, mothers can save, transact, and build financial independence without relying on traditional banks.
members.how_started: The idea was to send them small amounts of Bitcoin (SATS), around $5 weekly, to help with their babies daily need. However, we noticed that most of them were immediately converting the Bitcoin into Kenyan Shillings (Ksh) rather than using it directly. This raised a key question: What if they could spend Bitcoin without converting it? To make this possible, we began working with local businesses in Kimbo Matangi, encouraging them to accept Bitcoin as payment. The goal was to create a circular economy where Bitcoin could move from one person to another without leaving the community. Slowly, merchants started coming on board, and today, over 15 local businesses accept Bitcoin for goods and services.

members.name: EcoBitz
members.tagline: Building Kenya's Bitcoin circular economy through merchant onboarding and grassroots community education

members.name: Bitcoin Famba
members.tagline: Fostering Bitcoin adoption in Maputo, Mozambique through meetups, workshops, and Lightning education

members.name: College BTC
members.tagline: Empowering university students in Ghana to learn, earn, spend and build with Bitcoin through campus circular economies
members.description: College BTC is a youth-led Bitcoin movement bringing circular economies to university campuses. We empower students to learn, earn, spend, build, and spread Bitcoin in real life, from onboarding vendors and running meetups to teaching Bitcoin development and creating peer-to-peer opportunities. Our mission is simple: turn students into Bitcoin-powered builders, entrepreneurs, and leaders who shape the future of money and digital freedom
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

To raise a generation of students who are financially independent, digitally skilled, and empowered to build with Bitcoin — creating campus-based circular economies that grow into global youth-driven movements.

Our Mission

To spark a youth-led Bitcoin revolution on campuses by teaching Bitcoin development, empowering students to earn and spend in Bitcoin, and building real peer-to-peer economies that give young people financial freedom and a voice in shaping the future.
members.milestones: Milestones Onboarded 3 campus merchants to accept Bitcoin payments. Hosted a major campus-wide meetup introducing students to Bitcoin. Educated and orange-pilled 100+ university students on Bitcoin and financial independence. Officially accepted into the Bitcoin Confederations network. Successfully hosted 3 student-led Bitcoin meetups across universities.

members.name: TofoBTC
members.tagline: Bringing Bitcoin to Tofo beach community in Mozambique through merchant adoption and grassroots education

members.name: Soweto BTC
members.tagline: Building Bitcoin adoption in Soweto's township through merchant onboarding and education

members.name: Bitcoin Loxion
members.tagline: Empowering Khayelitsha township through Bitcoin education, merchant adoption, and a garden project with Bitcoin stipends
members.description: Bitcoin Loxion is a project focused on building a Bitcoin circular economy in one of the largest townships in South Africa. They are driven by a mission to empower the community, and provide decentralized opportunities that will help to bring about long term financial freedom.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

To transform one of South Africa's largest townships into a thriving hub of innovation and financial empowerment through a Bitcoin circular economy. They envision a community where every individual has access to decentralized opportunities, fostering resilience and collaboration.

Our Mission

To empower one of the largest South African townships by creating a Bitcoin circular economy. The project aims to transform lives through financial freedom and decentralized opportunities, by promoting a movement of community driven collaboration.
members.how_started: BitcoinLoxion began shortly after Adopting Bitcoin Cape Town. Initially, We held weekly meet-ups, but soon realized that many people lacked accurate information about Bitcoin. To address this, We partnered with Trezor Academic to offer classes in Khayelitsha. We also introduced the "My First Bitcoin" diploma program. In our efforts to establish a Bitcoin circular economy, we started a garden project where workers earn their stipends in Bitcoin, helping to circulate bitcoin within the community.
members.milestones: Our biggest milestone has been seeing people in Khayelitsha adopting Bitcoin. The real breakthrough came when local merchants started accepting Bitcoin payments for everyday needs. Additionally, harvest vegetables, selling our produce to the community, embracing Bitcoin as part of our business transactions. Emplimenting Bitcoin into daily life is a powerful step toward financial inclusion and economic empowerment in the area.

members.name: Bitcoin Karoo
members.tagline: Supporting Karoo's small-town businesses and residents with Bitcoin education and sound money principles
members.description: We are a circular economy based in the Karoo region in South Africa. A semi dessert area dominated by farming and agriculture. We are dispersed with small towns and owner managed businesses struggling under the pressure of a broken money system, rising costs, capital controls and regulation. Our project looks to educate merchants and residents in the principles of sound money and help guide and protect Karoo residents and businesses through the inevitable volatility and confusion of the transition to a global Bitcoin standard.
members.vision_mission: Basics principles we stand by: *We stand for self sovereignty *We stand for education first Our primary goals are: * Education on the global issue of fiat monetary debasement and the significant long term effects this has had on wealth distribution, cost of living, freedom and the growth of fiat based diseases. * Education on the core principles of the Bitcoin protocol and why this solves for these issues * Education on the safe accumulation and self custody of Bitcoin.

members.name: Bit-Fitness
members.tagline: Building Bitcoin awareness in Somerset West through fitness education and community health initiatives

members.name: Bitcoin Arusha
members.tagline: Building Bitcoin adoption in Arusha through cultural expression, combining education with music, dance, and events
members.description: Bitcoin Arusha: Revolutionizing Bitcoin Adoption Through Culture & Community Introduction Bitcoin Arusha is an innovative initiative designed to merge Bitcoin education with music, dance, and digital media, creating an immersive peer-to-peer (P2P) entertainment experience. By leveraging the power of art and community, we aim to cultivate a sustainable Bitcoin circular economy in Arusha. Our approach fosters real-world Bitcoin transactions while empowering artists, entrepreneurs, and professionals to integrate Bitcoin into their daily lives.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

&

Our Mission

Vision To establish Arusha as a thriving hub for Bitcoin adoption by integrating Bitcoin into cultural expression, commerce, and education. Mission To educate and empower individuals through accessible Bitcoin learning opportunities. To build a sustainable Bitcoin circular economy by encouraging real-world transactions. To connect professionals, artists, and businesses through strategic networking and events. To create an engaging cultural experience where Bitcoin fuels the exchange of creativity and value.
members.how_started: How It Began Inspired by Bitcoin Eksai and with the support and guidance from Bitcoin Kampala, Bitcoin Arusha was established in late 2024. Recognizing the need for practical Bitcoin use cases and awareness, we launched monthly educational classes and networking events to build a foundation for Bitcoin adoption in Arusha. By integrating Bitcoin with music, dance, and digital media, we are redefining how people interact with Bitcoin beyond investment—making it a functional tool for everyday transactions.
members.milestones: Milestones Bitcoin Education Launched – Monthly classes teaching 20-30 students the fundamentals of Bitcoin. Community Meetups Established – Regular gatherings of professionals and Bitcoin enthusiasts to foster knowledge-sharing and economic collaboration. Creative Content Development – Production of Bitcoin-themed music, dance, and visual storytelling to inspire and educate. Dance Battle Events Introduced – First pop-up battle event combining entertainment with real Bitcoin transactions. Annual Festival Announced – Planned flagship event bringing together top performers, Bitcoin merchants, and an expanded audience.

members.name: Bitcoin Kampala
members.tagline: Building Uganda's Bitcoin circular economy at Makerere University and supporting orphans through education and commerce
members.description: Bitcoin Kampala is building a grassroots Bitcoin circular economy in Uganda, centered around Makerere University—Uganda’s largest academic institution—and extending support to communities like the Orphans of Uganda in Bugiri. What began as a response to a tweet by Bitcoin Ekasi, calling for circular economies across Africa, has grown into a thriving initiative combining education, commerce, and social good. Since July 2023, Bitcoin Kampala has onboarded vendors, schools, students, and orphanages to use Bitcoin as a real medium of exchange. At Makerere University, students can now pay tuition and purchase supplies in Bitcoin, while merchants around campus participate in a growing Bitcoin economy. Programs like “Fruits and Eggs for Sats” introduce children to Bitcoin while ensuring proper nutrition. Beyond financial literacy, Bitcoin Kampala has secured land for farming, boosting food security and sustainability for orphans in Bugiri. Thanks to Bitcoin donations, the project has funded beds, desks, school materials, clean water systems, and renewable energy infrastructure. As adoption expands and partnerships grow, Bitcoin Kampala is proving that Bitcoin can be a tool for empowerment, financial sovereignty, and transformative impact across Uganda.
members.vision_mission: To create a sustainable and unrestricted bitcoin circular economy for locals.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Kampala Uganda was born out of the inspiration of Bitcoin Ekasi and Bitcoin Beach Brazil. A month prior to the BTC Prague 2023 conference, Bitcoin Ekasi issued a tweet challenging anyone who believed they could build a Bitcoin circular economy, drawing from the success of Bitcoin Beach in El Salvador. The offer was a conference ticket for BTC Prague, providing an opportunity for the right person to learn how to create a circular economy in their local community. Through a Twitter community poll, Brindon Mwiine B emerged as the winner, demonstrating his previous efforts in organising local Bitcoin meetups, educating the community about Bitcoin, and fostering adoption. He was awarded the conference ticket and embarked on a fundraising journey, utilising Geyser Fund, to cover his expenses for the trip to Prague. Against all odds, he succeeded in raising the necessary funds, getting a late visa and made it to the conference on the morning of the appointed day. Attending BTC Prague and immersing himself in the circular economies’ booth there proved invaluable. The knowledge and guidance he gained, particularly from the creators of Bitcoin Ekasi in South Africa and Bitcoin Beach Brazil, made the establishment of Bitcoin Kampala Uganda an inevitable and exciting endeavour.
members.milestones: Under the leadership of Brindon Mwiine, Bitcoin Kampala has significantly improved the lives of the orphaned children in Bugiri, providing essentials such as consistent schooling, daily nutritious meals, and the recurring “Fruits for Sats” program. At Makerere University, Bitcoin Kampala has established a thriving Bitcoin-friendly ecosystem, involving students, local vendors, and community stakeholders. The project has conducted over 8 Trezor Academy classes around Kampala and ensured mentorship and growth among active community members. Women's engagement in the local Bitcoin scene has greatly improved thanks to education initiatives by Bitcoin Dada, The Core, and now the School of Satoshi. Major achievements include: Educational Empowerment: Enabled orphaned children to regularly attend school, provided uniforms and materials, and created the foundation for the Starlight School project. Basic Needs Provision: Purchased 30 triple-layer beds, 90 mattresses, and 180 bedsheets for the orphanage. Community Initiatives: Onboarded vendors around Makerere University and launched Juicy B and Gorilla Sats to model Bitcoin adoption in businesses. Health and Nutrition: Improved diets through “Fruits for Sats” and “Sugar Free Juice” programs in Bugiri and Makerere. Self-Sustainability: Enabled crowdfunding campaigns for the orphanage and construction of a permanent home. Beyond Uganda: Helped launch Bitcoin Arusha in Tanzania, empowering a dancing community through Bitcoin.

members.name: Bitcoin Plett
members.tagline: Building Plettenberg Bay's Bitcoin circular economy with 100+ merchants and self-custody education principles
members.description: A forum to help guide and protect Plettenberg Bay residents and businesses through the inevitable volatility and confusion of the transition to a global Bitcoin standard. Basics: Take self custody. Bitcoin, not crypto! Seriously, Bitcoin only! Do not discuss price, predictions, or gambling/trading. Leave mining to the specialists. Evaluate ideas, not people. When in doubt, ask more questions. Feel free to advertise local businesses or events that promote and support Bitcoin education and awareness. Don't abuse the privilege. Stay humble, study Bitcoin, stack sats. Bring on the memes.

members.name: Bitcoin Ubuntu
members.tagline: Bitcoin-only education and onboarding in Swellendam, providing free resources and community events
members.description: Bitcoin Ubuntu is a volunteer organization in South Africa focused on Bitcoin-only education and onboarding. They offer free resources, consultation, and community events, emphasizing the philosophy of Ubuntu – "I am because we are" – highlighting the importance of community and shared growth.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

To empower individuals and communities through Bitcoin education, promoting financial literacy and inclusion. The group is inspired by the philosophy of Ubuntu, which highlights the value of a communal approach.

Our Mission

To provide free Bitcoin education and onboarding resources, through online education, consultations, meetups, merchant and charity onboarding, and running a Mi Primer Bitcoin light node. They seek to integrate the philosophy of Ubuntu into their educational efforts.
members.how_started: This volunteer organization was started in 2023 with a desire to provide free, high-quality, educational resources to the local community, so that they are better equipped to avoid fraudulent schemes and defend themselves against irresponsible government monetary and fiscal policies.
members.milestones: Bitcoin-Only Platform: Focused exclusively on Bitcoin, rather than other cryptocurrencies. Online Education: Provides free Bitcoin education through online resources. Consultation and Meetups: Offers guidance and community engagement through consultations and meetups. Merchant Onboarding: Helps local businesses adopt Bitcoin for transactions. Charity Onboarding: Works with charities to integrate Bitcoin into their operations. Mi Primer Bitcoin Light Node: Operates a light node and contributes to this open-source education network.

members.name: Bitcoin Witsand
members.tagline: Guiding Witsand's coastal village through the transition to a Bitcoin standard with education and self-custody
members.description: In the serene landscapes of the South Cape, Bitcoin Witsand stands as a transformative initiative empowering local communities and reshaping economies through Bitcoin adoption. Spearheaded by forward-thinking entrepreneurs, this project turns a scenic coastal village into a hub for decentralized financial systems, fostering inclusivity and financial independence.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

A thriving South Cape economy driven by financial empowerment, inclusivity, and technological innovation, showcasing Bitcoin as a sustainable medium of exchange.

Our Mission

To promote Bitcoin education and adoption within local communities, enabling businesses and individuals to leverage Bitcoin for everyday trade, savings, and economic resilience.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Witsand emerged as part of a broader movement inspired by Bitcoin Beach and Bitcoin Ekasi. These grassroots projects have laid the foundation for Bitcoin adoption in South Africa by demonstrating the practical use of Bitcoin in everyday transactions. Edwin Jones, a specialist programmer who relocated to Witsand, saw potential in transforming the area into a local Bitcoin economy. Beginning in 2022, through continuous education and community engagement, Jones successfully onboarded local businesses to accept Bitcoin as a preferred tender, proving its practicality in real-world scenarios.
members.milestones: 2021: Launch of Bitcoin Witsand, marking the start of a local Bitcoin economy in the South Cape. Onboarding Businesses: Many shops in Witsand now prefer Bitcoin payments, thanks to consistent education and outreach efforts. Partnerships and Growth: Collaborations with Bitcoin Ekasi and Bitcoin Ubuntu to expand the reach of Bitcoin education and adoption across the region.

members.name: Orphans of Uganda
members.tagline: Children's home caring for orphans in Bugiri, building a new home through Bitcoin crowdfunding and Fruits-for-Sats
members.description: Orphans of Uganda Children Center (OUCC) in Bugiri District is more than an orphanage; it is a family. Founded in 2009 and now guided by former resident Isma Byarugaba, the home nourishes, educates, and protects 77 children every day. In 2023 the Bitcoin Kampala community welcomed OUCC into the Bitcoin economy through the Fruits-for-Sats initiative, and a year later rallied again to save Starlight School. Early in 2025 Ray Youssef, CEO of the peer-to-peer platform NoOnes, donated 1 BTC, laying the foundation for a new home for these children. Now, with support from Bitcoin Kampala and Praia Bitcoin Brazil, we are running crowdfunding campaigns on Bitcoin Economy Confederation and Geyser.fund to bring this project to completion. Your sats are becoming bricks; help us lay the next one.
